title: Ship a Feature
subtitle: A small starting shape for one feature that needs intent, delivery focus, and a tool layer.
scope: One feature or product surface that needs to move from idea into working behavior.
sourceCoverage: Template only; replace placeholder feature references, tickets, and tool paths with real sources.

templateMeta.useWhen: Use this when you need a first living doc for a feature and want to stay focused on the main surface.
templateMeta.operatingThesis: Start with the main product surface and its delivery tooling; grow only when design drift or verification pressure becomes structurally important.

semanticDefinition.objectiveRole: Seed a feature delivery doc with a primary surface and the tooling needed to work on it.
semanticDefinition.templateSubtitle: A small starting shape for one feature that needs intent, delivery focus, and a tool layer.
semanticDefinition.templateScope: One feature or product surface that needs to move from idea into working behavior.
semanticDefinition.templateObjective: Make the main feature legible enough that a teammate can see what it is, what matters most, and how to work on it.
semanticDefinition.templateSuccessCondition: A reader can name the primary surface, the current delivery target, and the tools used to update or render the doc.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.role: Summarize feature delivery readiness without replacing the surface or tooling cards.
sections.rationale: This section gives a small feature doc a quick readiness signal while the actual feature and tool evidence stays separate.

sections.title: Design–Code–Spec Flow
sections.role: Name the primary feature surface as a joined design, code, spec, interaction, and ticket entity.
sections.rationale: A feature needs one concrete surface card before delivery work can be understood or handed off.

sections.title: Tooling Surface
sections.role: Expose the scripts, workflows, and artifacts used to update or render the feature doc.
sections.rationale: A teammate needs an operational tool path to keep the feature doc current instead of only reading the feature card.

relationships.rationale: Feature status should summarize the real surface being shipped.
The feature surface is operational only when the update/render tools are visible.

stageSignals.question: What primary feature surface should this starter doc make tangible first?

stageSignals.when: feature surface exists but tooling-surface has no tool path
stageSignals.question: Which script, workflow, or artifact lets a teammate update or render this feature doc?

stageSignals.when: feature surface and tooling are populated
stageSignals.question: Can a teammate name the feature, current delivery target, and tools from this doc?

validOperations.label: Add feature surface
validOperations.description: Add the primary design-code-spec feature card.

validOperations.label: Add feature tooling
validOperations.description: Add a tool card that explains how to update, render, or inspect the feature doc.

objective: Make the main feature legible enough that a teammate can see what it is, what matters most, and how to work on it.
successCondition: A reader can name the primary surface, the current delivery target, and the tools used to update or render the doc.

callouts.title: How to use this starter
items: Begin with one primary surface instead of trying to map every edge case immediately.
Add alignment and verification sections only when the feature needs that extra structure.

sections.title: Status Snapshot

callout.title: Starter intent
items: Keep the opening section short and obvious.
Use it to show what the feature is and where attention should go next.

sections.rationale: This section gives a small feature doc a quick readiness signal while the actual feature and tool evidence stays separate.

sections.title: Design–Code–Spec Flow

data.name: Primary surface
notes: Start with the single surface that would make the feature feel real if it worked.

sections.rationale: A feature needs one concrete surface card before delivery work can be understood or handed off.

sections.title: Tooling Surface

data.name: Universal renderer
data.purpose: Turn the canonical JSON into a standalone HTML snapshot that can be shared with other people.
data.whenToUse: Use whenever the living doc needs to leave your local editing context.
caveats: Rendered HTML is portable, but the JSON remains the canonical version to keep current.

sections.rationale: A teammate needs an operational tool path to keep the feature doc current instead of only reading the feature card.
